name: Namecheap API
what_it_does: Namecheap's XML/REST API for domain registration, DNS management, WHOIS lookup, SSL certificate management, and domain portfolio management.
use_cases: Managing DNS records for Namecheap-hosted domains
Bulk domain availability checking for domain acquisition workflows
SSL certificate management and renewal automation
not_for: Domains registered elsewhere (use their registrar's API)
CDN and security features (use Cloudflare instead)
High-frequency DNS changes (Cloudflare DNS is faster)
best_when: You manage domains through Namecheap and need to automate domain registration, renewal, or DNS management.
avoid_when: You need advanced DNS features, CDN, or fast propagation — Cloudflare is better for DNS management.

auth.notes: API key plus username passed as query parameters. IP whitelist required — API calls only accepted from pre-approved IP addresses. No OAuth or scoped tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free with Namecheap account
pricing.paid_tiers: Domain registration costs; API access itself is free
pricing.notes: API access free but requires minimum $50 in account or 20+ domains for activation. Must have IP whitelisting configured. Sandbox environment available for testing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: XML responses with error codes and messages. Error codes are numeric and require lookup in documentation. Messages not always descriptive enough for self-diagnosis.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Domain check and DNS read operations are idempotent. Registration and DNS changes are not.
known_agent_gotchas: API requires IP whitelist — agents must be running from a known static IP, no dynamic cloud runners
Response format is XML, not JSON — requires XML parsing
API activation requires minimum account balance or domain count — new accounts may not have access
Rate limit of 20 calls/minute is very restrictive for bulk domain operations
Sandbox environment uses different endpoint (api.sandbox.namecheap.com) — don't mix up with production
DNS management through Namecheap API is slower to propagate than Cloudflare-based DNS
